name: kitcounterpartygrid
description: Project containing your PowerApps Component Framework (PCF) control.

comments.virtual: This is a virtual control: the platform hands it the host's own React and Fluent at runtime (the manifest's platform-library lines), so neither is bundled and the grid's popovers run on the host page's single focus-management instance, no pin needed. React and Fluent live in devDependencies purely for compile and local harness use. The Fluent version here is the kit's API floor (9.61.0, the oldest release exporting SearchBox, which the grid's search bar uses), higher than the 9.46.2 the manifest declares: the manifest number is only the version the org must ACCEPT at import (the runtime serves its current copy), while this number is the oldest delivery the code actually works against. Compiling against it keeps the control off APIs an older target org might not serve.
comments.icons: The icon package is not part of the platform Fluent library, so it stays a real dependency; webpack.config.js aliases it so the shared source outside this project bundles the same single copy.
